description: Every checkpoint outcome (approved, rejected, edited, aborted) is logged to .bober/audits/<runId>.jsonl with structured ApprovalRecord. Enables replay, compliance review, debugging of feedback loops. Audit log is append-only; runs that crash mid-stream leave the audit log in a recoverable state.

successCriteria.description: src/orchestrator/checkpoints/audit.ts exports recordApproval(runId, record): Promise<void>. Each call appends one JSON line to .bober/audits/<runId>.jsonl with shape: {timestamp, runId, checkpointId, mechanism, outcome, approverId, iteration, feedbackText?, editDeltaSummary?, durationMs}.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read audit.ts. Verify ApprovalRecord shape. Test: call recordApproval, verify .jsonl line.

successCriteria.description: All three mechanisms (CLI, disk, PR) record an approval entry on every outcome. The CheckpointMechanism interface includes a hook that the orchestrator calls in a finally block — guarantees the audit is recorded even on mechanism error.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep mechanism files for audit.recordApproval calls. Verify each is in a finally or try-catch path. Test: simulate mechanism throwing — verify audit entry still written.

successCriteria.description: Append-only: audit.ts uses fs.appendFile (or stream-append) — never overwrites. Concurrent appends from multiple async checkpoints serialize via in-process mutex.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read audit.ts. Verify appendFile usage (not writeFile). Verify mutex. Test: 100 parallel recordApproval calls produce 100 distinct lines.

successCriteria.description: approverId resolution: from $USER env var, then `git config user.name`, then 'unknown'. PR mechanism uses the GitHub username from the comment/merge actor.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read audit.ts and PR mechanism. Verify the resolution chain.

successCriteria.description: Edit delta summary: when outcome is 'edit', editDeltaSummary contains the line count of the delta and the first 200 chars (not the full delta — that's stored elsewhere). Full delta path referenced in the entry.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: edit outcome → audit entry has editDeltaSummary with line count and char-truncated preview.

successCriteria.description: Replay tool: `bober audit show <runId>` CLI subcommand prints a human-readable table of all approval records for that run (timestamp, checkpoint, outcome, approver, iteration).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Verify CLI subcommand. Test: produce an audit log, invoke the command, verify table output.

successCriteria.description: Audit file is created with permissions that allow user read but not world-read (mode 0600 on POSIX); approverId may include sensitive identifiers.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file-creation logic. Verify mode 0600. Test: stat the created file, verify permissions.

successCriteria.description: Unit tests cover: (a) approval, rejection, edit, abort each produce a distinct entry, (b) mechanism-error path still writes entry, (c) concurrent appends produce distinct lines, (d) approverId fallback chain, (e) audit show command.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Locate tests. Run npm run test. Verify all branches.

expectedChanges.description: Directory for per-run audit JSONL files

generatorNotes:
JSONL is the right format: append-only, line-oriented, easy to grep, easy to replay. Each record is self-contained JSON on one line. Never spans lines.

ApprovalRecord shape (complete):
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-05-24T15:30:00.123Z",
  "runId": "run-20260524-153000",
  "checkpointId": "post-research-spec-20260524-foo-1",
  "mechanism": "disk" | "cli" | "pr" | "noop",
  "outcome": "approved" | "rejected" | "edited" | "aborted",
  "approverId": "oleksii" | "github:obra" | "unknown",
  "iteration": 1,
  "feedbackText": "first 500 chars of feedback if any",
  "editDeltaSummary": { "lineCount": 12, "firstChars": "..." } | null,
  "durationMs": 12345
}
```

In-process mutex: use a simple Promise chain — every recordApproval enqueues behind the previous. With async appendFile under the hood, this serializes writes.

```typescript
let writeChain: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve();
export function recordApproval(runId, record): Promise<void> {
  const next = writeChain.then(() => fs.appendFile(...));
  writeChain = next.catch(() => {}); // don't block subsequent on prior errors
  return next;
}
```

Mechanism integration: wrap each mechanism's request() body in a try-finally that calls recordApproval with the outcome. Mechanism errors still record an entry (outcome: 'aborted', errorMessage in feedbackText).

approverId resolution chain (precise order):
1. PR mechanism: GitHub user from comment/merge event
2. CLI mechanism: process.env.USER || process.env.USERNAME
3. disk mechanism: try git config user.name, fallback to env USER
4. noop mechanism: 'autopilot'
5. fallback for all: 'unknown'

File permissions: fs.open with mode 0o600 on creation. For append, the existing mode is preserved by appendFile.

`bober audit show <runId>`: read .bober/audits/<runId>.jsonl, parse each line, render as a table:
```
Timestamp                Checkpoint                       Outcome     Approver     Iter   Duration
2026-05-24T15:30:00.123  post-research-...                approved    oleksii      1      45.2s
2026-05-24T15:31:00.456  post-plan-...                    rejected    oleksii      1      12.1s
2026-05-24T15:35:00.789  post-plan-...                    approved    oleksii      2      3m12s
```

Support `--json` flag for machine-readable output.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify file mode 0600 is set on CREATION (not just when chmod'd later — race condition). Use fs.open with O_CREAT|O_APPEND|O_WRONLY and mode arg, then write.

Verify concurrent-append test actually exercises concurrency: 100 setImmediate-scheduled appendFile calls should NOT produce partial lines or interleaved JSON. The mutex must prevent this.

Verify mechanism-error path: a test that throws inside a mechanism's request() must still produce an audit entry (outcome: aborted, with error message). If the test passes without the throw producing an entry, the finally block isn't wired correctly.

Verify approverId resolution chain: a test should mock each fallback in sequence and assert correct attribution. Document the chain in audit.ts comments so it's auditable later.

Verify NO sensitive data leaks into the audit log unexpectedly: feedbackText is truncated to 500 chars — verify this in test. editDeltaSummary should NOT include the full delta — that's stored elsewhere. The audit file is mode 0600 BUT it's still on disk and may be backed up; assume it's eventually readable.

The `bober audit show` command should work even if .bober/audits/<runId>.jsonl doesn't exist (print 'No audit log found for run X' and exit non-zero). Don't crash with ENOENT.
